title: Multiple Colour Combinations Well-plate Imaging

steps: Power the microscope and open NIS elements software

Place the well-plate onto the stage holder, select an objective and focus on the first well to be image with the required magnification (e.g. 4x objective). Note: for scanning large numbers of wells an air objective or water objective with water immersion dispenser is advised.
open 'JOBS' explorer, click 'new' and then 'import'. Import the JOB (in material section of this protocol) from wherever you have saved the .bin file.
Run the JOB by clicking the 'play' icon next to the JOB in the JOB explorer window. This will open the JOB GUI and guide you through setting up the colors and rows to be imaged.

Select the color combinations required for each row of the well-plate by selecting combinations of optical configurations. Note that the first capture definition (contain multiple or single OCs) will be used on the first row and so on.
Define the well-plate being used by selecting from the database in NIS and align the well-plate by following the guidance in the alignment GUI.
Select the rows or columns of wells to be imaged with each successive capture definition.
Click 'run' Note by clicking 'edit' on the JOB prior to running, annotations on what each step contains and how these can be duplicated to add >3 color combination and >3 rows / columns can be found:
creator: Joe Airey
